meta.name: 50-Mile Trail Ultra
meta.subtitle: Your first 50-mile ultramarathon in 24 weeks
meta.description: A 24-week trail ultramarathon plan built around progressive long runs, back-to-back weekend sessions, and elevation-focused training. For runners who have completed a marathon and want to step up to the ultra distance.
meta.coach_notes: Trail ultras are about time on feet, not pace. The back-to-back long runs on Saturday and Sunday are the cornerstone of ultra preparation — they train your body to run on already-depleted glycogen stores, which is what racing a 50-miler requires. Practise your nutrition and gear every single long run.

weeks.title: Base week 1
weeks.note: First week. The back-to-back format (Saturday + Sunday long runs) is the signature of ultra training. Keep Saturday easy enough that Sunday is also possible.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hill repeats · 6x3min uphill at effort, jog down · Total 10km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
20min easy threshold · 5:10–5:25/km — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
18km on trail · Easy effort · Practice carrying nutrition — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

sessions.det: 12km on trail · Easy · No targets · Time on feet — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

weeks.title: Base week 2
weeks.note: Saturday back-to-back grows. Practise eating during runs — gel every 45 minutes, water every 30.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Hill repeats · 8x3min uphill (jog down) · Total 11km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
25min threshold — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

20km trail · Easy · Gel every 45min — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

sessions.det: 14km trail · Easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

weeks.title: Base week 3
weeks.note: 22km Saturday run — the long run is approaching proper ultra training distance.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

VO2max · 5x1km (3min rec) · Total 11km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
30min threshold — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

22km trail · Elevation focus if available — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

weeks.title: Development 3
weeks.note: 28km back-to-back. This weekend of running is your first taste of the total fatigue an ultra creates.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
Hill repeats · 12x3min · Total 13km — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.
35min threshold — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
28km trail — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
12km trail · Easy · Gear check — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

weeks.title: Race week — 50-mile trail ultra
weeks.note: Race week. Rest. Every kilometre you run this week is a kilometre wasted.

sessions.det: 3km easy · Strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
3km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Walk every significant uphill from mile 1 · Eat at every aid station · Gel every 45min between stations · Move steadily through the night · Crew at every permitted point · The finish is everything — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Large bowl of porridge with banana and nut butter. 800ml water. Salt tablet if sweating heavily. This breakfast powers your first 4–5 hours.
Gel or real food at every aid station. Target 60–80g carbohydrates per hour. At 50 miles, running out of fuel is your biggest risk — eat even when you do not feel hungry.
Water and electrolytes at every aid station. Salt tablets every 60–90 minutes in warm conditions. Check urine colour — pale yellow is correct.
At crew/drop bag stations: broth, potatoes with salt, banana, rice balls, peanut butter sandwich. Real food absorbs better than gels at ultra distances.
Caffeine gel or tablet after 12 hours or when drowsy. Do not rely on caffeine before this point — save it for when you need it.
Isotonic drink immediately. 40g protein within 30 minutes. Full meal within 2 hours. Continue eating every 2–3 hours for 24 hours post-race. Your glycogen stores are deeply depleted.
